evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG/TAP requester driving tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i
evidence_slots.operation: read/write DMI registers and receive DMI responses via DMI op encoding and valid/ready handshake
evidence_slots.object: debug_req/debug_resp DMI interface through dmi_jtag/dmi_cdc into the debug module
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMI request valid generation and response exposure should be gated by successful JTAG authentication/unlock, not merely TAP DMI selection/update events.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag sets jtag_unlock_o equal to combinational pass_check; pass_check is defaulted low each combinational evaluation and is not a persistent unlock register. The DMI read path accepts an update when `(pass_check | ~we_flag == 1)`, so reads are allowed if the top-level we_flag_4 is 0. Read/Write states assert dmi_req_valid, and capture/shift exposes dr_q through dmi_tdo/TDO when DMIACCESS is selected. dmi_cdc only transports the valid/data handshakes and does not add an authorization filter.
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals external JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS -> dmi_jtag FSM/DR -> dmi_cdc -> debug_req/debug_resp -> debug module
evidence_slots.state_condition: After TRST, state_q is Idle, error_q is DMINoError, and DMI read acceptance depends on pass_check or inverse we_flag. When DMIACCESS is selected, capture/shift can return response data on TDO even without a persistent unlock state.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthenticated external JTAG traffic can potentially read debug module DMI state and observe debug responses, enabling debug capability probing and information disclosure.

rtl_evidence.detail: Authentication-related pass_check/pass_mode/startHash/hashValid signals are local logic, not shown as persistent unlock state in the evidence.
Tool-observed model record: jtag_unlock_o is assigned pass_check; pass_check defaults low; DMI read transition condition uses `(pass_check | ~we_flag == 1)` while writes require pass_check.
Tool-observed model record: dmi_req_valid is asserted in Read/Write states.
dmi_tdo is dr_q[0]; capture/shift loads and shifts DMI DR contents when dmi_access is selected.
dmi_cdc connects internal DMI request/response to dmi_req_valid_o/dmi_resp_ready_o.
cdc_2phase transfers DMI request/response valid/data; no auth gate is present.
i_dmi_jtag connects to debug_req/debug_resp and receives we_flag_4.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: External JTAG DMI read/response path is not consistently gated by a persistent authenticated unlock state; DMI reads can be accepted when we_flag is deasserted and response data is shifted to TDO under DMIACCESS without an unlock check.
inspection_records.notes: Finding is specific to visible RTL gating; downstream debug module authorization was not shown to compensate.

evidence_slots.subject: software/NOC/MMIO requester reaching the PKT/fuse path
evidence_slots.object: fuse_mem rdata_o and the jtag_hash_o/ikey_hash_o/okey_hash_o material used by JTAG HMAC authentication
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Fuse/hash material used for JTAG authentication should not be readable over ordinary software-visible/MMIO paths unless protected by privilege/lifecycle/access-control checks and secret-range filtering.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: fuse_mem contains constants labeled JTAG expected HMAC hash, HMAC okey/ikey hashes, HMAC key, access-control words, SHA/AES keys, and returns mem[addr_q] on rdata_o for any in-range requested address. riscv_peripherals connects pkt_wrapper fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata to fuse_mem and also wires jtag_hash/ikey_hash/okey_hash from the same fuse memory to dmi_jtag.
evidence_slots.path: NOC PKT channel -> noc_axilite_bridge -> pkt_wrapper -> fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata -> fuse_mem.mem -> jtag_hash/ikey_hash/okey_hash -> dmi_jtag
evidence_slots.state_condition: On a clock where req_i is high, addr_q is captured from addr_i; rdata_o returns the addressed mem word for in-range addresses. No secret-range filter is visible in fuse_mem.
evidence_slots.impact: A requester able to drive the PKT/fuse read path can disclose JTAG authentication reference material and embedded crypto constants, undermining the JTAG authentication scheme and exposing secrets.

rtl_evidence.detail: mem contains words labeled JTAG expected HMAC hash.
mem contains HMAC okey hash, ikey hash, and key-like constants.
mem includes SHA/AES key material.
Tool-observed model record: req_i captures arbitrary in-range addr_i, rdata_o returns mem[addr_q], and jtag_hash_o/okey_hash_o/ikey_hash_o are built from the same mem array.
pkt_wrapper exposes fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o/fuse_rdata_i and receives acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i.
Tool-observed model record: fuse_mem connects to fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata and jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: The fuse memory stores JTAG authentication hash/key material in the same array returned by the generic fuse read port, with no filtering visible in fuse_mem.
inspection_records.notes: pkt_wrapper receives access-control inputs, but the provided evidence does not show it filtering secret fuse addresses before returning fuse_rdata.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible NOC/MMIO requester, especially the debug NOC requester
evidence_slots.object: debug module slave register path and security-sensitive peripheral wrappers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Security-sensitive peripheral and debug accesses should be mediated by acc_ctrl/priv_lvl_i/debug_mode_i/reglk_ctrl or equivalent checks before accept/ready/read-data behavior.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals derives acc_ctrl_c from acc_ctrl and priv_lvl_i and passes acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i/debug_mode_i to many wrappers. However, the debug NOC bridge maps directly to dm_master, axi2mem converts dm_master into dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata, and dm_top receives those slave signals without visible top-level acc_ctrl, priv_lvl_i, debug_mode_i, or reglk_ctrl qualification.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_debug_noc* -> i_debug_axilite_bridge -> dm_master AXI -> axi2mem -> dm_slave_* -> dm_top
evidence_slots.state_condition: For crypto/reset/fuse/DMA wrappers, protection depends on wrapper-internal use of provided acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i. For the debug NOC path, no visible top-level permission signal gates the request path before dm_top.
evidence_slots.impact: Ordinary NOC/MMIO traffic on the debug channel may access debug module registers and potentially affect halt/reset/debug state without the access-control checks applied to other protected peripherals.

rtl_evidence.detail: reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl are defined; acc_ctrl_c is derived from acc_ctrl and privilege index.
axi2mem converts dm_master bus to dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata.
debug NOC axilite bridge connects directly to dm_master signals.
Tool-observed model record: dm_slave_* feeds dm_top slave interface directly.
Other wrappers receive reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and in several cases debug_mode_i or PMP inputs.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Most security-sensitive wrappers receive acc_ctrl/reglk/debug inputs, but the debug NOC path is bridged directly into the debug module slave interface without visible top-level acc_ctrl/privilege/debug-mode mediation.

evidence_slots.subject: software/NOC requester on reglk channel and external we_flag_0..we_flag_4 sources
evidence_slots.operation: set/clear lock bits and write locked registers
evidence_slots.object: reglk_ctrl output from reglk_wrapper and protected wrapper reglk_ctrl_i inputs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Lock bits should be safe on reset, sticky or only authorized-clearable, and should gate every protected write path including alternate external flags.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals instantiates reglk_wrapper producing reglk_ctrl and passes slices of reglk_ctrl to protected wrappers. AES0 ORs its lock slice with we_flag_1; DMA receives we_flag_3; dmi_jtag receives we_flag_4. The provided evidence does not include reglk_wrapper internals or update equations, so sticky behavior, authorized clear, and exact semantics of we_flag bypass/lock cannot be confirmed.
evidence_slots.path: reglk NOC channel -> reglk_axilite_bridge -> reglk_wrapper -> reglk_ctrl -> protected wrappers and external we_flag inputs
evidence_slots.state_condition: Need reglk_wrapper reset/update equations and wrapper write-gate semantics to determine if lock bits can be cleared or bypassed.

rtl_evidence.detail: reglk_wrapper outputs reglk_ctrl and receives acct_ctrl_i, jtag_unlock, and rst_9.
reglk NOC axilite bridge drives reglk_axi_req/resp.
Protected wrappers receive reglk_ctrl_i slices; AES0 receives `reglk_ctrl[...] | we_flag_1`.
DMA receives reglk_ctrl_i plus separate we_flag_3.
dmi_jtag receives we_flag_4.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: reglk_wrapper source, reset value of reglk_ctrl, whether reglk_ctrl supports 1->0 transitions, semantics of external we_flag_* inputs, and final protected-register write gates.
inspection_records.notes: Missing evidence request identified impact; because the core sticky/bypass behavior is not established from provided observations, no vulnerability impact is asserted.

evidence_slots.subject: test/JTAG interface
evidence_slots.operation: select DMI instruction, shift/capture/update, drive TDO
evidence_slots.object: dmi_jtag_tap DMI access controls and TDO output path
evidence_slots.expected_guard: testmode_i should not enable DMI access, bypass authentication, or expose debug data except under intended authorization.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In the visible TAP RTL, testmode_i selects only the DFT TCK clock mux between inverted TCK and TCK. DMIACCESS selection, DTMCSR, TDO muxing, shift/capture/update generation, and TDO output enable are driven by TAP state and IR selection rather than by testmode_i. No testmode-specific debug access bypass is visible.
evidence_slots.path: dmi_jtag_tap testmode_i -> pulp_clock_mux2 for tck_n; TAP FSM/IR/DR path remains controlled by JTAG state and instruction selection
evidence_slots.state_condition: When testmode_i is asserted, tck_n uses tck_i instead of inverted tck_ni; TDO still updates in the same always_ff and tdo_oe_o remains `(shift_ir | shift_dr_o)`. DMIACCESS response exposure is a general JTAG-auth issue captured by PERM-JTAG-DMI-AUTH-001, not a testmode-specific bypass in the provided evidence.

rtl_evidence.detail: tdo_mux selects IDCODE, DTMCSR, DMIACCESS dmi_tdo_i, or BYPASS based on IR; no testmode condition.
testmode_i is used as clk_sel_i for DFT clock mux only.
td_o and tdo_oe_o update on tck_n; tdo_oe_o depends on shift_ir or shift_dr_o, not testmode.
TAP FSM generates capture/shift/update controls from tap_state_q/tms_i, not testmode.

inspection_records.notes: Missing evidence request identified impact, but no testmode-specific candidate violation is supported by the provided observations.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG requester
evidence_slots.operation: reset/clear/activate debug interface through DTMCS dmireset/dmihardreset/TRST
evidence_slots.object: DMI reset, authentication state, and debug reset/activity outputs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: JTAG-controlled DMI reset should not clear auth/lock into an unlocked condition or allow debug reset/control operations without normal authorization.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag assigns dmi_rst_no directly to rst_ni; in riscv_peripherals the dmi_rst_no output is unconnected. dmi_reset from dmi_jtag_tap is used in dmi_jtag only to clear the sticky DMI error when dtmcs_select is active. TRST resets dmi_jtag state to Idle and error to DMINoError. The visible reset path does not assert dmactive_o, ndmreset_o, or debug_req_o directly and does not set a persistent unlocked state.
evidence_slots.path: DTMCSR dmireset/dmihardreset in dmi_jtag_tap -> dmi_reset -> dmi_jtag error clear; dmi_rst_no=rst_ni but unconnected at top
evidence_slots.state_condition: On TRST, dr_q/address_q/data_q reset to zero, state_q becomes Idle, and error_q becomes DMINoError. dmi_reset with dtmcs_select clears DMI error only in the provided evidence.

rtl_evidence.detail: dmi_rst_no is assigned rst_ni.
dmi_reset && dtmcs_select clears error_d to DMINoError.
TRST resets DR/state/address/data/error; no unlock reset-to-true behavior visible.
dmi_rst_no from i_dmi_jtag is left unconnected.
DTMCS contains dmihardreset/dmireset fields.

inspection_records.notes: This does not mitigate the broader DMI auth gating weakness recorded in PERM-JTAG-DMI-AUTH-001.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG requester controlling DMI scan data
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Authentication should depend on a valid completed HMAC/hash comparison against fuse-provided reference, with no stale hash, bypass, or partial-load condition permitting unlock.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag has HMAC-related pass_data/exp_hash/pass_hash/startHash/hmac_ready/hashValid signals and instantiates hmac. hmac supports key_hash_bypass_i, which causes precomputed ikey/okey hash blocks to be used. The provided model record indicates pass_check is combinationally defaulted low, jtag_unlock_o is assigned directly to pass_check, and DMI read acceptance can depend on inverse we_flag rather than a persistent hashValid-authenticated unlock. This means the visible unlock/DMI policy is not a robust completed-HMAC authorization state.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG DMI scan data -> dmi_jtag pass_data/startHash -> hmac -> pass_hash/hashValid/hmac_ready -> pass_check/jtag_unlock_o -> DMI access FSM
evidence_slots.state_condition: On reset, HMAC ready/hash_valid reset low in hmac. During normal operation, key_hash_bypass_i can select precomputed hash block mode. Need the full dmi_jtag PassChk/PassChkValid lines to confirm exact comparison timing, but DMI access is already not consistently tied to persistent hashValid.
evidence_slots.impact: A non-persistent or bypassed authentication decision can allow failed, partial, stale, or policy-disabled authentication to leave DMI access reachable, weakening JTAG debug authentication.

rtl_evidence.detail: FSM includes PassChk/PassChkWait/PassChkValid states and HMAC/auth signals pass_check, pass_mode, startHash, exp_hash/pass_hash, pass_data, hmac_ready, hashValid.
hmac inputs include key_i, ikey_hash_i, okey_hash_i, key_hash_bypass_i, message_i; outputs include hash_o, ready_o, hash_valid_o.
On reset hmac clears state/hash_o/ready_o/hash_valid_o; init_i starts hashing and clears hash_valid_o.
key_hash_bypass_i controls whether SHA init or precomputed hash-block update is used for the inner key block.
dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash, okey_hash, ikey_hash and outputs jtag_unlock.
Fuse memory stores JTAG expected HMAC hash and HMAC key/hash material.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full line-level comparison equations in dmi_jtag PassChk/PassChkValid and exact key_hash_bypass_i connection value in the hmac instance were not fully visible in provided observations.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: The JTAG authentication decision appears transient and not tied to a persistent valid completed HMAC comparison before DMI access decisions; HMAC bypass-related sequencing remains risky in combination with pass_check/jtag_unlock wiring.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate is supported primarily by the observed non-persistent pass_check/jtag_unlock/DMI policy and HMAC bypass-capable datapath; exact bypass controllability remains uncertain.

evidence_slots.subject: DMA engine and software configuring DMA
evidence_slots.object: DMA wrapper/master path and protected memory/peripheral address regions
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMA configuration and transfers should be constrained by access-control/range/PMP checks and should not race active transfers to escape bounds.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The top-level instantiates dma_wrapper and provides reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i derived from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8], pmpcfg_i, pmpaddr_i, and we_flag_3. The NOC DMA channel is bridged to dma_axi_req/resp. The provided observations do not include dma_wrapper internals or the DMA master transaction path/range-check equations, so adequacy of DMA address-range enforcement cannot be determined.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_dma_noc* -> i_dma_axilite_bridge -> dma_axi_req/resp -> dma_wrapper with acct_ctrl/reglk/PMP inputs
evidence_slots.state_condition: Need DMA wrapper behavior for configuration writes under privilege/access bits, PMP/range enforcement, and active-transfer config races.

rtl_evidence.detail: dma_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, pmpcfg_i, pmpaddr_i, we_flag_3, axi_req_i/axi_resp_o, and rst_8.
DMA NOC axilite bridge connects buf_ariane_dma_noc* to dma_axi_req/resp.
DMA AXI-lite sideband signals are tied to fixed defaults.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: dma_wrapper internals, master egress path, PMP/range-comparison equations, active-transfer state behavior, and security impact of any missing range checks.
inspection_records.notes: Missing evidence request identified impact; no candidate violation is asserted without the internal DMA range-check evidence.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG requester or software/NOC debug requester
evidence_slots.operation: write/control debug module state to trigger halt/debug request/reset/debug activation
evidence_slots.object: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o and debug module control state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Debug requests, dmactive, and ndmreset should require debug authorization and should not be triggerable by unprivileged MMIO/NOC traffic or unauthenticated JTAG.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals connects i_dmi_jtag to debug_req/debug_resp and also connects the debug NOC bridge through dm_master/axi2mem into dm_top's slave interface. The debug NOC bridge path lacks visible top-level acc_ctrl/priv_lvl/debug_mode/reglk qualification. The JTAG path has the authentication weakness described in PERM-JTAG-DMI-AUTH-001. dm_top outputs ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, and debug_req_o at the top level.
evidence_slots.path: (1) external JTAG -> dmi_jtag -> dmi_cdc -> debug_req/debug_resp -> dm_top; (2) debug NOC -> i_debug_axilite_bridge -> dm_master -> axi2mem -> dm_slave_* -> dm_top -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: Risk applies when NOC debug requester can drive the debug channel or when JTAG we_flag/auth state allows DMI access. Reset defaults/persistence of dmactive inside dm_top were not inspected in the provided observations.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthorized requester control of debug-module registers may halt harts, activate debug mode, or assert non-debug-module reset, causing privilege escalation, denial of service, or security-state manipulation.

rtl_evidence.detail: Top-level outputs ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, and debug_req_o are exposed.
i_dmi_jtag connects to debug_req_valid/debug_resp_valid.
axi2mem produces dm_slave_* from dm_master.
debug NOC axilite bridge drives dm_master directly.
Tool-observed model record: dm_slave_* feeds dm_top.
DMI read/response gating weakness described for JTAG path.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: dm_top internal authorization behavior is not included; however top-level debug NOC and JTAG ingress guards are visibly weak or absent.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Visible requester paths to the debug module include an unguarded debug NOC slave path and a JTAG DMI path with weak authentication gating, creating a plausible unauthorized debug-control route.

evidence_slots.subject: software/NOC reset requester
evidence_slots.object: rst_1..rst_13 controlled peripheral resets and affected security state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Software-triggered resets of security-sensitive peripherals should be authorized and should not reset lock/access-control/authentication state into a permissive condition.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals declares rst_1 through rst_13 and instantiates a reset wrapper that receives reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, debug_mode_i, and rst_12. Individual wrappers receive rst_* inputs. The provided observations do not include reset wrapper internals, reset register write decode, rst_* mapping for all security-sensitive modules, or post-reset state defaults, so reset permission behavior cannot be determined.
evidence_slots.path: reset NOC channel -> rst axilite bridge -> reset wrapper -> rst_1..rst_13 -> protected peripheral wrappers/security state
evidence_slots.state_condition: Need write conditions for reset-control registers, mapping of rst_* to protected peripherals, and whether resets affect reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl/JTAG auth/debug state permissively.

rtl_evidence.detail: rst_1..rst_13 signals are declared.
Tool-observed search/model record: reset wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, and debug_mode_i.
Reset AXI request sideband tie-offs are visible near reset section.
Examples of security-sensitive wrappers receiving rst_13, rst_1, rst_9, and rst_8.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: reset wrapper source/update equations, authorization checks for reset writes, full rst_* mapping, and security impact of post-reset defaults/residue.
inspection_records.notes: Missing evidence request identified impact; no candidate violation is asserted without reset-control internals.
